content.cn_text: 现在，请仔细听并回答31至40题。
content.en_text: Now, listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 早上好。
content.en_text: Good morning.

content.cn_text: 我们很高兴我们大学将举办一场音乐会，演奏这位音乐家的作品
content.en_text: We're really pleased that our university is going to hold a concert featuring the music

content.cn_text: 意大利作曲家萨尔瓦托雷·斯切里诺的。
content.en_text: of the Italian composer Salvatore Scherino.

content.cn_text: 他被认为是欧洲现代古典音乐的领军作曲家之一。
content.en_text: He's considered one of the leading composers of modern classical music in Europe.

content.cn_text: 他的音乐非常不寻常，所以为了让大家为这场音乐会做好准备，我将
content.en_text: His music is highly unusual, so in order to prepare you for this concert, I'm going to

content.cn_text: 谈谈他的生平和音乐。
content.en_text: talk about his life and music.

content.cn_text: 首先，一些生平细节。
content.en_text: First, some biographical details.

content.cn_text: 萨尔瓦托雷·斯切里诺于1947年出生在意大利城市巴勒莫。
content.en_text: Salvatore Scherino was born in the Italian city of Palermo in 1947.

content.cn_text: 尽管他确实接受过一些音乐课程，但他主要是一名自学成才的作曲家。
content.en_text: Though he did receive some music lessons, he is mainly self-taught as a composer.

content.cn_text: 在他的家乡读了几年大学后，他于1969年搬到罗马，在那里他学习了
content.en_text: After a few years of university in his home city, he moved to Rome in 1969, where he studied

content.cn_text: 电子音乐。
content.en_text: electronic music.

content.cn_text: 这段经历成为斯切里诺作为作曲家发展的一个转折点。
content.en_text: This experience served as a turning point in the development of Scherino as a composer.

content.cn_text: 谈到他的作品，他在1970年代创作了大量作品，并在1982年之前担任音乐教师。
content.en_text: Turning to his work, he composed a great deal in the 1970s, and was a music teacher
until 1982.

content.cn_text: 除了作曲和教学，他还创作了大量散文，内容涉及
content.en_text: But from composing music and teaching, he has also produced a large number of essays on

content.cn_text: 一系列主题，如其作品背后的哲学和音乐形式。
content.en_text: a range of topics such as the philosophy behind his work and musical form.

content.cn_text: 在他的整个职业生涯中，他获得无数享有盛誉的奖项，最著名的是2011年的一项大奖，
content.en_text: Throughout his career, he is one numerous prestigious awards, most notably a major prize

content.cn_text: 该奖项表彰了他在音乐上的原创性。
content.en_text: in 2011, which recognized his musical originality.

content.cn_text: 所以可以说他是我们时代最重要的作曲家之一。
content.en_text: So you could say that he is one of the most important composers of our times.

content.cn_text: 现在，我将谈谈他的音乐特点，这让一些人有点
content.en_text: Now, I'll talk about the characteristics of his music, which some people are a bit

content.cn_text: 惊讶。
content.en_text: surprised by.

content.cn_text: 许多听众会首先注意到斯切里诺音乐的特点是频繁
content.en_text: The first thing that many listeners will notice about Scherino's music is the frequent

content.cn_text: 使用寂静。
content.en_text: use of silence.

content.cn_text: 他的音乐另一个显著特点是他偏爱极其柔和的声音
content.en_text: What is also striking about his music is his preference for extremely soft sounds

content.cn_text: 有时音量低到几乎听不见。
content.en_text: that are sometimes at such a low volume that they can hardly be heard.

content.cn_text: 他的音乐另一个不寻常的方面是，他经常要求音乐家演奏他们的
content.en_text: The other unusual aspect of his music is that he frequently requires musicians to play their

content.cn_text: 乐器以非传统的方式，因此，例如，一把小提琴最终可能会发出
content.en_text: instruments in unconventional ways, so that, for example, a violin may end up sounding

content.cn_text: 非常奇怪的声音，并创造出我们通常与大自然相关联的声音。
content.en_text: very strange, and creating sounds we generally associate with nature.

content.cn_text: 实际上，他新颖的器乐技巧经常被其他作曲家模仿。
content.en_text: Actually, his novel instrumental techniques have often been imitated by other composers.

content.cn_text: 因此，他被广泛认为是创造新声音方面的先驱。
content.en_text: As a result, he's widely regarded as a pioneer in terms of creating new sounds.

content.cn_text: 这听起来可能很奇怪，但斯切里诺本人将他的音乐比作一座从远处火山
content.en_text: It may sound strange, but Scherino himself has likened his music to a volcano viewed from

content.cn_text: 看去。
content.en_text: a far.

content.cn_text: 正如你所看到的，它缓慢地积蓄，达到激动人心的高潮。
content.en_text: As you'll see, it builds up slowly to an exciting climax.

content.cn_text: 让我们继续讨论我们将在音乐会上听到的那首特别作品。
content.en_text: Let's move on to talk about the particular piece that we'll hear at the concert.

content.cn_text: 许多人认为这是他迄今为止最优秀的作品。
content.en_text: Many people consider it to be his finest work to date.

content.cn_text: 这是一部宏大的钢琴、合唱团和管弦乐队作品，于1979年完成。
content.en_text: It's an immense piece of music for piano, chorus, and orchestra that was completed in 1979.

content.cn_text: 这部作品献给一位意外去世的朋友，所以它是一种
content.en_text: The work is dedicated to a friend who died unexpectedly, so it was written as a kind

content.cn_text: 纪念。
content.en_text: of memorial.

content.cn_text: 在许多方面，它代表了他的整体作品在声音方面的
content.en_text: In many ways, it is representative of his work as a whole in terms of the sound that

content.cn_text: 创造。
content.en_text: it creates.

content.cn_text: 它在技术上令人望而生畏，对音乐家来说是一部极具挑战性的作品。
content.en_text: It's technically formidable, and it's a very challenging piece for musicians to play.

content.cn_text: 这首曲子以一种极具复杂性的低沉声音开始，类似于雷声。
content.en_text: The piece opens with a low sound of great complexity that is similar to thunder.

content.cn_text: 这种低音噪音几乎不间断地持续了45分钟，这可能听起来
content.en_text: This low-pitched noise is sustained almost uninterrupted for 45 minutes, which may sound

content.cn_text: 相当无聊，但我发现它非常令人难以忘怀和富有氛围感。
content.en_text: quite boring, but I found it to be very haunting and atmospheric.

content.cn_text: 顺便说一下，这种声音是由大的振动金属片产生的，这些金属片被两个
content.en_text: The sound, by the way, is created by large vibrating sheets of metal, which are slowly manipulated

content.cn_text: 打击乐手缓慢操作。
content.en_text: by two percussionists.

content.cn_text: 有弦乐器产生的非常安静和奇特的和弦，以及非常复杂的钢琴
content.en_text: There are very quiet and strange chords produced by string instruments and very complex piano

content.cn_text: 旋律，它们悄然出现然后消失。
content.en_text: melodies that quietly appear and then disappear.

content.cn_text: 尽管这首曲子对许多人来说，在它所创造的声音方面非常不寻常，
content.en_text: Even though the piece will be for many people very unusual in terms of the sounds it creates,

content.cn_text: 它实际上却有一个非常传统的结构，这仅仅是斯切里诺作品的另一个令人惊讶的方面。
content.en_text: it actually has a very conventional structure, which is just another surprising aspect of

content.cn_text: 斯切里诺的作品。
content.en_text: Skierino's work.

content.cn_text: 现在我们来看另一部作品。
content.en_text: Now let's move on to another work.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of part four.

content.cn_text: 现在你有一分钟时间检查第四部分的答案。
content.en_text: You now have one minute to check your answers to part four.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31-40.

items.title: As well as music, he has written many 31. ___________.
He has won many awards – including one in 2011 for 32. ___________ in music.
Surprisingly, his music often features 33. ___________ and very soft sounds.
His music uses unusual instrument techniques, e.g. violins often produce sounds which resemble those found in 34. ___________.
He is seen as a 35. ___________ of music.
Sciarrino compares his music to a distant 36. ___________.
finished in 1979 and written in memory of a 37. ___________.
starts with a noise that sounds like 38. ___________, produced by two people moving large pieces of 39. ___________
starts with a noise that sounds like 38. ___________, produced by two people moving large pieces of 39. ___________
although the music is unusual, it has a traditional 40. ___________

Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
The Music of Salvatore Sciarrino
Biographical details
• He was born in Palermo in 1947.
• He moved to Rome in 1969 and took a course in electronic music, which influenced his style as a composer.
Work
• In the 1970s he composed a lot of music and taught until 1982.
• As well as music, he has written many $31$.
• He has won many awards – including one in 2011 for $32$ in music.
Musical characteristics
• Surprisingly, his music often features $33$ and very soft sounds.
• His music uses unusual instrument techniques, e.g. violins often produce sounds which resemble those found in $34$.
• He is seen as a $35$ of music.
• Sciarrino compares his music to a distant $36$.
Concert piece
• for piano, chorus and orchestra
• finished in 1979 and written in memory of a $37$.
• very difficult to play
• starts with a noise that sounds like $38$, produced by two people moving large pieces of $39$
• although the music is unusual, it has a traditional $40$
